paragraph.heading: Delivery order contract.—
paragraph.chapeau: The term “delivery order contract” means a contract for property that—

subparagraph.content: does not procure or specify a firm quantity of property (other than a minimum or maximum quantity); and
provides for the issuance of orders for the delivery of property during the period of the contract.

paragraph.heading: Task order contract.—
paragraph.chapeau: The term “task order contract” means a contract for services that—

subparagraph.content: does not procure or specify a firm quantity of services (other than a minimum or maximum quantity); and
provides for the issuance of orders for the performance of tasks during the period of the contract.

html:tr.style: font-size:6pt; border-bottom:1px solid black; border-top:1px solid black; -uslm-lc:h1;

html:th.style: min-width: 44.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black;
width:68.0pt ; max-width:68.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black;
width:94.0pt ; max-width:94.0pt; text-align:center; vertical-align:middle; border-left:1px solid black;

html:td.style: text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-right:1px solid black; border-left:1px solid black; padding-right:2pt; padding-left: 2pt;
text-align:left; vertical-align:top; border-left:1px solid black; padding-left: 2pt;
html:td.html:p: June 30, 1949, ch. 288, title III, § 303K, as added Pub. L. 103–355, title I, § 1054(a), Oct. 13, 1994, 108 Stat. 3265.
